children.heading: Preliminary review by hearing officer
children.label: a Preliminary review by hearing officer

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: Not later than 7 days after transmission to the employing office of a claim pursuant to , the Executive Director shall appoint a hearing officer to conduct a preliminary review of the claim.section 1402(b) of this title

children.heading: Process for appointment
children.label: 2 Process for appointment
children.text: The Executive Director shall appoint a hearing officer under this subsection in the same manner and in accordance with the same requirements and procedures applicable to the appointment of a hearing officer under .section 1405(c) of this title
Whether the claimant is a covered employee authorized to obtain relief relating to the claim under this subchapter.
Whether the office which is the subject of the claim is an employing office under this chapter.
Whether the individual filing the claim has met the applicable deadlines for filing the claim under this subchapter.
The identification of factual and legal issues involved with respect to the claim.
The specific relief sought by the individual.

Whether, on the basis of the assessments made under paragraphs (1) through (5), the individual filing the claim is a covered employee who has stated a claim for which, if the allegations contained in the claim are true, relief may be granted under this subchapter.
The potential for the settlement of the claim without a formal hearing as provided under or a civil action as provided under .section 1405 of this titlesection 1408 of this title

children.heading: Report
children.label: 1 Report
children.text: Not later than 30 days after a claim is filed under , the hearing officer shall submit to the individual filing the claim and the office which is the subject of the claim a report on the preliminary review conducted under this section, and shall include in the report the hearing officer’s determination as to whether the individual is a covered employee who has stated a claim for which relief may be granted under this subchapter (as described in paragraph (6) of subsection (b)). The submission of the report shall conclude the preliminary review.section 1402 of this title

children.heading: Extension of deadline
children.label: 2 Extension of deadline
children.text: The hearing officer may (upon notice to the individual filing the claim and the employing office which is the subject of the claim) use an additional period of not to exceed 30 days to conclude the preliminary review.

children.heading: Effect of determination of failure to state claim for which relief may be granted
children.label: d Effect of determination of failure to state claim for which relief may be granted

children.text: the individual (including an individual who is a Library claimant, as defined in ) may not obtain a formal hearing with respect to the claim as provided under ; andsection 1401(d)(1) of this titlesection 1405 of this title

the hearing officer shall provide the individual and the Executive Director with a written notice that the individual may file a civil action with respect to the claim in accordance with .section 1408 of this title

children.heading: Transmission of report on preliminary review of certain claims to congressional ethics committees
children.label: e Transmission of report on preliminary review of certain claims to congressional ethics committees

children.text: the Committee on Ethics of the House of Representatives, in the case of such an act by a Member of the House of Representatives (including a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to the Congress); or
the Select Committee on Ethics of the Senate, in the case of such an act by a Senator.
